text: ‘Shall find a blessing’; some commentators would interpret this, ‘shall bless themselves in thy name’, that is, use it as a proverbial instance of prosperity.

It seems that Thare was still alive at the time of Abram’s migration (cf. 11.26, 32). St Jerome, assuming that Thare was dead, would date Abram’s age of seventy-five not from his birth, but from the time of his leaving Ur.

‘The Valley of Clear Seeing’; in the Hebrew text, ‘The oak of Moré’ (a proper name). The Latin version evidently follows a different tradition, which would connect the name of the place with the Hebrew word for ‘appearance’ (cf. the verse following).
